plan.goal: Address 10 code review findings from Phase 2 second review
plan.context: Phase 2 second code review found 10 issues (4 medium, 6 low) across wflib/record.py, wflib/log.py, wflib/worktree.py, wflib/git.py, wflib/types.py, and tests/integration/. The codebase has 288 passing tests, 133 skipped (Phase 3+). `tests/integration/helpers.py` already provides a shared `init_repo(base_dir, with_commit=True)` helper. `wflib/types.py` is the dependency root — all other wflib modules import from it. `record.py` has a private `_now_iso()` that normalizes timestamps to Z-suffix.

tasks.title: Extract shared timestamp utility, fix float precision, add list_records test, clean up imports
tasks.goal: Address findings 1, 5, 9, 10. (1) Extract the Z-suffix timestamp function from record.py into a shared location and have both record.py and log.py use it. (2) Remove unused imports from test_record.py or mark them with a comment explaining they're coverage reminders. (3) Round cost after accumulation in get_total_usage. (4) Add a test for list_records that verifies it returns valid records and skips malformed files with a warning.
constraints: Create a utc_now_iso() function accessible to both log.py and record.py — either in a tiny wflib/_util.py or by making _now_iso public in record.py and importing it in log.py. Prefer _util.py to avoid circular import risk.
log.py must produce Z-suffix timestamps matching record.py after the change
In get_total_usage, apply round(total.cost, 6) before returning
The list_records test should create a directory with 2 valid records and 1 malformed .json file, assert 2 records returned, and verify a warning was written to stderr

For unused imports in test_record.py: remove the ones that have no test (get_implementation_state, list_records import is now used by the new test, record_implementation_start, record_review) OR keep them with a '# used by test below' / '# no dedicated test yet — exercised in E2E' comment. Prefer removing truly unused ones.
acceptance: python3 -m pytest tests/ --tb=short passes with no regressions (288+ passed)
grep -rn 'utc_now_iso\|_now_iso' shows a single definition used by both log.py and record.py
log.py timestamps end with Z, not +00:00

tasks.title: Extract commit helper, harden hook execution, type merge_state, document close cleanup
tasks.goal: Address findings 2, 3, 7, 8. (1) Extract the repeated stage+check-staged+commit pattern in worktree.py into a _stage_and_commit helper. (2) Add capture_output, timeout, and proper error wrapping to the .worktree-setup hook subprocess call. (3) Add a MergeState Literal type for merge_state/merge_result fields. (4) Add a comment to close_workflow_worktree noting cleanup is the caller's responsibility.
constraints: The _stage_and_commit(cwd, add_args, commit_args) helper should encapsulate: git add with add_args, check staged files, git commit with commit_args, raise RuntimeError on failure, return bool
commit_if_dirty, commit_or_amend_workflow_files, and commit_remaining_changes must all delegate to this helper
setup_worktree hook execution must use capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=120, and catch CalledProcessError to raise RuntimeError with the hook's stderr
Add MergeState = Literal['clean', 'conflicted', 'failed'] to types.py and use it for CloseRecord.merge_result and WorkflowCloseResult.merge_state
Add a comment at the end of close_workflow_worktree noting that worktree+branch cleanup is the caller's responsibility (like wf close), matching the merge_back pattern
acceptance: python3 -m pytest tests/ --tb=short passes with no regressions
grep -c '_stage_and_commit' wflib/worktree.py shows 4+ hits (1 def + 3 callers)
grep 'MergeState' wflib/types.py shows the Literal definition

tasks.title: Add git.py porcelain comment and fix test init_repo duplication
tasks.goal: Address findings 4 and 6. (1) Add a comment in get_dirty_files explaining the --porcelain column layout and rename handling. (2) Replace the manual repo setup in test_commit_or_amend_workflow_files_no_prior_commits with a call to init_repo(Path(tmp_dir), with_commit=False) from the shared helpers module.
constraints: The comment in get_dirty_files should explain: porcelain format is 'XY <path>' with path starting at column 3, renames show 'XY old -> new', and we take the destination path after '->'
The test must import init_repo from tests.integration.helpers and use init_repo(Path(tmp_dir), with_commit=False) — remove all manual subprocess calls for repo setup
Do not change test assertions or behavior — only the setup method
acceptance: python3 -m pytest tests/ --tb=short passes with no regressions
grep -c 'subprocess.run' tests/integration/test_worktree.py shows fewer hits than before (manual init_repo calls removed)
grep -A3 'porcelain' wflib/git.py shows the format comment

task-3.title: Add git.py porcelain comment and fix test init_repo duplication
task-3.summary: Added a comment in `get_dirty_files` explaining the `--porcelain` column layout and rename handling, and replaced the manual repo setup in `test_commit_or_amend_workflow_files_no_prior_commits` with `init_repo(Path(tmp_dir), with_commit=False)`.
task-3.notes: The `subprocess.run` count in `test_worktree.py` dropped from 29 to 25, confirming the 4 manual init calls were removed. Test assertions and behavior are unchanged.
task-3.diffStat:
tests/integration/test_worktree.py | 16 +---------------
 wflib/git.py                       |  3 +++
 2 files changed, 4 insertions(+), 15 deletions(-)

task-1.title: Extract shared timestamp utility, fix float precision, add list_records test, clean up imports
task-1.summary: Addressed all four findings: extracted `utc_now_iso()` into `wflib/_util.py` shared by both `record.py` and `log.py`, cleaned up unused imports in `test_record.py` (removed `get_implementation_state`, `record_implementation_start`, `record_review`), added `round(total.cost, 6)` in `get_total_usage`, and added a `TestListRecords` test that verifies valid records are returned and malformed files produce a stderr warning. Tests: 290 passed (up from 289), 133 skipped.
task-1.notes:
- Kept `_now_iso = utc_now_iso` alias in `record.py` to avoid touching every internal call site — all ~14 usages continue working through the alias, and there's a single canonical definition in `_util.py`.
- The `round(total.cost, 6)` fix addresses float accumulation drift (e.g., `0.1 + 0.2 = 0.30000000000000004`).

task-2.title: Extract commit helper, harden hook execution, type merge_state, document close cleanup
task-2.summary: Extracted `_stage_and_commit(cwd, add_args, commit_args)` helper in worktree.py (used by 3 callers), hardened `.worktree-setup` hook execution with `capture_output`/`text`/`timeout`/error wrapping, added `MergeState = Literal['clean', 'conflicted', 'failed']` type alias in types.py (used in both `CloseRecord` and `WorkflowCloseResult`), and documented cleanup responsibility in `close_workflow_worktree`'s docstring.
